title: The Swan King | Ch 9 | Roots and Wings Class 6 | Jataka Tales | Difficult words in description box
description:
In this video, I am going to explain chapter 9, The Swan King of Class 6th Roots and Wings literature reader book. 

DIFFICULT WORDS 
1. Kingdom - a country, state, or territory ruled by a king or queen.(राज्य)
2. Noble - belonging by rank, title, or birth to the aristocracy.(महान)
3. Hunter - a person or animal that hunts. (शिकारी)
4. Far-off - remote in time or space.(दूर)
5. Different - not the same as another or each other; unlike(अलग)
6. Marshy Land - waterlogged (धँसाऊ)
7. Suitable - right or appropriate (उचित)
8. Bushes - Thick Grass (झाड़ियाँ)
9. Clan - a large family (कुल)
10. Dutiful - Responsible (आज्ञाकारी)
11. Devoted - very loving or loyal (भक्त)
12. Grazing - eat grass in a field. (चराई)
13. Habitat - the natural home (घर)
14. Trapped - catch (फंस गया)
15. Dissuade - persuade not to take a particular course of action. (न करने के लिए समझाना)
16. Dangerous - able or likely to cause harm or injury. (खतरनाक)
17. Trap - catch an animal (फँसाना)
18. Lured - tempt (लालच)
19. Insisted - demand something forcefully (जोर दिया)
20. Ultimately - finally (अंतिम रूप में)
21. Gliding - floating 
22. Descended - go down
23. Bruises - Injury (चोटें)
24. Adequate - satisfactory (पर्याप्त)
25. Strength - state of being physically strong. (शक्ति)
26. Sake - for the purpose of (खातिर)
27. Entangled - caught in (उलझा हुआ)
28. Enduring - suffer (सहनीय)
29. Trouble - difficulty (मुसीबत)
30. Robust-Looking - strong (हट्टा कट्टा)
31. Relish - like (पसंद)
32. Prey - hunt and kill for food. (शिकार)
33. Loosened - make less tight or firm.(ढीला)
34. Grateful - thankful (आभारी)
35. Concern - worry (चिंता)
36. Persuasion - convincing (समझाना-बुझाना)
37. Appreciation - recognition
38. Revenue - income (आय)
39. Palatial - palace-like (आलीशान)
40. Chariot - a two-wheeled vehicle drawn by horses (रथ)
41. Sermon - a talk on a religious or moral subject (प्रवचन)
42. Generosity - being kind and generous (उदारता)
43. Bade - utter
